evals.name: cohesive-feature
evals.prompt: I just finished adding the password strength meter to the signup form. Can you commit these changes? Go ahead and stage and commit them — no need to wait for explicit confirmation, just do it as if I said yes to your plan.
evals.expected_output: ONE commit grouping the password meter component, its test, and the signup form integration. Conventional-commits format with a body that explains why the password meter exists (likely security/UX). No co-author trailer.

evals.name: mixed-unrelated
evals.prompt: Commit everything in the working tree. Treat my approval as implicit — go ahead and run the git commands once you've grouped them. Just show me what you did at the end.
evals.expected_output: THREE separate commits: (1) the new dashboard widget feature with its test, (2) the typo fix in the unrelated README, (3) the dependency bump in package.json + lockfile. Each with its own Conventional Commits message and a body explaining the why.

evals.name: secrets-and-cruft
evals.expected_output: The skill should: (a) flag the .env file as a likely secret and refuse to auto-stage it, (b) ask about or skip the untracked scratch files (notes.txt, debug.log), (c) separate the lint-only formatting sweep (formatting.ts) into its own commit, and (d) commit the real feature change as a focused commit. No co-author trailer anywhere.
